Package cz.zcu.kiv.brada.primitui

Class Show

java.lang.Object
cz.zcu.kiv.brada.primitui.Show
public class Show extends Object
Static utility class for writing simple formatted output to the console.

This class provides small helper methods used by the library to print lines, headers, horizontal rules, newlines and formatted strings. All methods are static convenience wrappers around System.out.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static void
    clear()
    Attempt to clear the console by emitting ANSI escape sequences and flushing the output.
    static void
    err(Object msg)
    Print the string representation of the parameter to stderr, without a trailing newline.
    static void
    errf(String format, Object... args)
    Print a formatted error message to stderr.
    static void
    errln(Object msg)
    Print the string representation of the parameter to stderr, adding a newline.
    static void
    header(String header)
    Print a section header framed with equals signs, followed by a newline.
    static void
    header(String header, char frameChar)
    Print a section header framed with the given character.
    static void
    hr()
    Print a horizontal rule (separator line).
    static void
    nl()
    Print a single newline to the console.
    static void
    text(Object text)
    Print the given object's string representation to the console without a trailing newline.
    static void
    textf(String fmt, Object... args)
    Print a formatted string to the console using System.out.printf().
    static void
    textln(Object text)
    Print the given object's string representation to the console followed by a newline.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • text

      public static void text(Object text)
      Print the given object's string representation to the console without a trailing newline.
      Parameters:
      text - the object for which `toString()` is called to print (may be an empty string)

    • textln

      public static void textln(Object text)
      Print the given object's string representation to the console followed by a newline.
      Parameters:
      text - the object for which `toString()` is called to print (may be an empty string)

    • textf

      public static void textf(String fmt, Object... args)
      Print a formatted string to the console using System.out.printf().
      Parameters:
      fmt - the format string (see Formatter)
      args - format arguments referenced by the format specifiers in fmt

    • header

      public static void header(String header)
      Print a section header framed with equals signs, followed by a newline.
      Parameters:
      header - the header text to print

    • header

      public static void header(String header, char frameChar)
      Print a section header framed with the given character.
      Parameters:
      header - the header text to print
      frameChar - the character to use for framing

    • hr

      public static void hr()
      Print a horizontal rule (separator line).

    • nl

      public static void nl()
      Print a single newline to the console.

    • err

      public static void err(Object msg)
      Print the string representation of the parameter to stderr, without a trailing newline.
      Parameters:
      msg - the object for which `toString()` is called to print (may be an empty string)

    • errln

      public static void errln(Object msg)
      Print the string representation of the parameter to stderr, adding a newline.
      Parameters:
      msg - the object for which `toString()` is called to print (may be an empty string)

    • errf

      public static void errf(String format, Object... args)
      Print a formatted error message to stderr.
      Parameters:
      format - the format string (see Formatter)
      args - format arguments referenced by the format specifiers in text

    • clear

      public static void clear()
      Attempt to clear the console by emitting ANSI escape sequences and flushing the output. This works in most ANSI-capable terminals but is not guaranteed on all platforms or IDE consoles.
